topic: Cursor MCP v2.1 Setup: Full Tool Discovery and Server Cards Configuration
keyword: cursor mcp v2.1 setup server cards

key_points: Covers three setup methods: UI, mcp.json project-scoped, and global config
Addresses security vulnerabilities CVE-2025-54136 (MCPoison) and CVE-2025-54135 (CurXecute)
Details stdio vs Streamable HTTP transport comparison
Recommends least-privilege credentials for MCP server integrations
Official docs: Settings > Tools & MCP > New MCP Server UI
Agent mode (Cmd+I) required for MCP tool invocation
Green/yellow/red status indicators for server health
Project config (.cursor/mcp.json) takes precedence over global (~/.cursor/mcp.json)
Step-by-step walkthrough from install to first tool call
Explains one-click deep-link installation from server docs pages
Covers dynamic context management update (January 2026): 47% token reduction
Recommends capping active tools at ~40 to prevent agent degradation
Focus on popular server integrations: GitHub, PostgreSQL, Figma, Slack
Details full quit-and-reopen requirement after server install
Explains mcpServers root key requirement (silent failure if missing)
Covers project vs global scope strategy for different server types
Explains Cursor Marketplace launched February 17, 2026 (Cursor 2.5)
Covers MCP Apps support for interactive UIs in Cursor 2.6 (March 2026)
Team marketplace for Enterprise/Teams plan admins
Authorization flow: yellow dot = needs Connect click to complete setup

stats.fact: MCP reached 97 million monthly downloads by March 2026, a 970x increase in 18 months
67% of CTOs surveyed in Q1 2026 say MCP is or will be their default agent-integration standard within 12 months
9,400 published MCP servers across four major registries as of Q2 2026, with +58% QoQ growth rate sustained three quarters in a row
January 2026 Cursor update introduced dynamic context management across MCP servers, reducing token usage by 47% when using multiple servers simultaneously
MCP Server Cards reduce initial connection latency by 30% in environments with 100+ MCP servers by pre-fetching server capabilities
~40 active tools is the recommended ceiling in Cursor — beyond that the agent picks the wrong tool due to context pressure
Only 12.9% of MCP servers score 'high trust' (70+/100), meeting quality thresholds for documentation, maintenance, and reliability
Stdio transport achieves 10,000+ operations per second — an order of magnitude faster than HTTP-based transports under typical conditions

angles: Server Cards are the v2.1 killer feature — they let registries and clients auto-discover server capabilities via .well-known/mcp.json without connecting first, cutting setup friction dramatically
Security angle: after CVE-2025-54136 (MCPoison) and CVE-2025-54135 (CurXecute) the approval-every-tool-call default was enforced in Cursor 2.0 — readers need to understand what changed
Transport choice is now a real architectural decision: stdio for local/single-dev setups, Streamable HTTP for team-shared or cloud-deployed servers
The 40-tool ceiling is counterintuitive — more installed servers feels like more power, but actually degrades agent accuracy; a curated set beats a maximalist one
Cursor Marketplace (since Feb 2026) makes MCP onboarding a one-click experience for most popular servers, eliminating manual JSON editing for mainstream use cases
Enterprise angle: team marketplaces let admins push pre-approved MCP configs org-wide, replacing per-developer mcp.json juggling
keywords: how to add mcp server cursor ide
cursor mcp tool discovery agent mode
mcp stdio vs streamable http cursor
cursor mcp global vs project config

H2: Enabling Full Tool Discovery in Agent Mode
H2: Choosing a Transport: stdio vs Streamable HTTP
